# Validation corpus

The Ogg files and ffmpeg f32le reference files are generated by
`tool/generate_fixtures.dart`. They cover mono/stereo streams, 8/22.05/44.1/48
kHz, low/high quality settings, tones, deterministic white noise, transients,
silence, a licensed recorded-piano phrase, a one-audio-packet empty stream,
an eight-frame non-empty stream, and a ten-second stream.

`manifest.json` records exact hashes, encoder/decoder versions, packet counts,
ffmpeg-vs-stb RMS/max errors, and the fixed limits that the Dart decoder must
meet. Reference lengths must match exactly; the comparator never trims or
aligns outputs to make a comparison pass.

ffmpeg 8.1.1 normally applies packet skip/discard metadata in a way that omits
valid tail samples for these libvorbis fixtures. Reference generation decodes
with `+skip_manual`, removes the initial priming region measured from the
difference between default and manual first-frame PTS values, and trims at the
known final-granule frame count. The result matches stb_vorbis/libvorbis
sample-for-sample in length. This does not relax the Dart check: its output
must independently have the exact manifest length.

Normal tests need only the committed files. Regeneration additionally needs
ffmpeg, ffprobe, oggenc, a C compiler, and network access the first time the
pinned `stb_vorbis.c` is cached. Set `STB_VORBIS_SOURCE` to a local copy for an
offline audit.
